channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Federal Government to Reserve 20% of Gas for Domestic Use from 2027
item.description: The federal government has unveiled a significant policy change requiring gas exporters to reserve 20 per cent of their production for Australian consumers. This measure, modelled on Western Australia...

item.title: Recent political cartoons by Simon Letch featured in SMH gallery
item.description: This Sydney Morning Herald feature presents a curated gallery of recent political cartoons by Australian illustrator Simon Letch. The collection showcases Letch’s distinctive visual style and sharp co...

item.title: Man arrested after allegedly threatening Prince Andrew near Sandringham residence
item.description: Prince Andrew was reportedly left shaken after an encounter with a man wearing a balaclava near his new residence on the Sandringham estate in Norfolk. The incident took place at around 7.30pm on Wedn...

item.title: Implications of Abolishing the BSA on Media Standards in New Zealand
item.description: The New Zealand government, led by Media and Communications Minister Paul Goldsmith, has announced the abolition of the Broadcasting Standards Authority (BSA), raising concerns about the enforceabilit...

item.title: Australian women and children with links to Islamic State face legal proceedings after returning from Syria
item.description: A group of 13 Australians, including women and children with alleged links to Islamic State, returned to Australia from Syria this week. Janai Safar, 32, has been charged in Sydney with entering a dec...

item.title: Reflections on OP Sindoor One Year On and What to Expect Next
item.description: The article reflects on the one-year milestone of the OP Sindoor initiative, examining its progress, challenges faced, and the potential implications of a second phase. Over the past year, OP Sindoor ...

item.title: Tanzania Prepares for 2026/27 Sesame Season Focusing on Quality Amid Global Market Challenges
item.description: Tanzania is set to open the 2026/27 sesame trading season on May 8, 2026 in Songwe Region. Farmers expect higher production this year thanks to government efforts through COPRA to provide better seeds...

item.title: National Government Accelerates Preparations for First Regular BARMM Parliamentary Elections
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. announced that the national government is moving at full speed to prepare for the first regular parliamentary elections in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Min...

item.title: Israel attacks southern Beirut targeting Hezbollah leader amid fragile ceasefire
item.description: Israel has hit Beirut on Wednesday, marking the first airstrike since the ceasefire with Hezbollah was agreed last month. Israeli authorities said the target was a commander from Hezbollah’s elite Rad...

item.title: Africa underrepresented in global medical clinical trials
item.description: Modern medicine dey claim say e universal and based on data from clinical trials, but study don show say Africa dey largely left out. Africa get around 19% of world population and carry 25% of global ...

item.title: Mudavadi Discloses Rising Kenyan Casualties in Russia-Ukraine Conflict
item.description: Prime Cabinet Secretary Musalia Mudavadi has come out with serious updates on the plight of Kenyan nationals trapped in the Russia-Ukraine war. According to him, at least 19 Kenyans have already lost ...

item.title: Young South Africans recognised for shaping Africa’s future through ideas and innovation
item.description: On 5 May 2026, the South African Bureau of Standards (SABS) in Pretoria hosted the announcement of the national winners of the 2025/2026 African Organisation for Standardisation (ARSO) Essay Competiti...

item.title: Psilocybin Reduces Aggression in Mangrove Rivulus Fish Without Affecting Social Behavior
item.description: A recent study published in Frontiers in Behavioral Neuroscience investigated how psilocybin, the psychoactive compound found in many magic mushrooms, affects the social behavior of mangrove rivulus f...

item.title: Police investigate incident where car targeted Jewish schoolchildren in Finchley
item.description: An investigation has been launched after a black car reportedly drove towards three Jewish boys from Hasmonean High School in Finchley, north-west London, on 20 April at approximately 15:40 BST. The i...

item.title: DG Khan school roof collapses: Four students dead, 20 hurt, investigation underway
item.description: In a tragic incident in Dera Ghazi Khan, Punjab, four students lost their lives and twenty others were injured when the roof of The Little Scholars School collapsed on Thursday around 11:52am. Rescue ...

item.title: Lawyer claims evidence against Mabanta lacks strength
item.description: Franco Mabanta's legal team has publicly stated that the case being filed against their client is weak and lacks sufficient evidence. According to Mabanta's lawyer, the allegations do not present a st...

item.title: Pregnant Woman Loses Baby at Lapai General Hospital Due to Generator Gas Shortage During Caesarean Section
item.description: One pregnant woman for Lapai General Hospital for Lapai, Niger State, lose her unborn baby on April 17, 2026, because the hospital generator gas run out during her caesarean section operation. The thi...

item.title: Trump Optimistic as Iran Considers US Peace Proposal, Talks Continue
item.description: US President Donald Trump expressed optimism about ending the ongoing conflict with Iran quickly, following Tehran's review of a US peace proposal. Sources close to the mediation said the proposed one...

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos AI Model Dramatically Improves Firefox Bug Detection
item.description: In April 2026, Anthropic introduced its Mythos AI model, a system capable of detecting high-severity software vulnerabilities with unprecedented efficiency. Mozilla’s Firefox security team has since r...

item.title: Nairobi Official Rescues 14-Year-Old Girl From Alleged Forced Marriage
item.description: On the night of Wednesday, May 6, Nairobi County Chief Officer for Citizen Engagement and Customer Service, Geoffrey Mosiria, carried out a rescue operation for a 14-year-old Ethiopian girl living in ...
